event: Virgin and Child Enthroned with Saints
symbols: enthroned Virgin with child — Mary as the Seat of Wisdom and Throne of God
Saint Jerome with book and bare chest — the scholar-ascetic devoted to Scripture
female saint with book — learning and devotion in service of the faith
columns and architectural frame — the Church as the structured setting of the holy encounter
description: A standing Virgin Mary in a blue mantle over a red dress holds the Christ child on her arm, elevated on a pedestal between two marble columns. At left, a young female saint in dark robes holds a book. At right, an elderly bearded man in a red draped robe, bare-chested, reads from a book — identifiable as Saint Jerome. The architectural setting features classical columns and a decorated entablature. An inscription appears on the pedestal base. The figures are rendered with Renaissance precision and gravity.
theological_description: This sacra conversazione presents the Virgin and Child flanked by saints in a composition that embodies the theology of heavenly communion. The enthroned Madonna, elevated on a pedestal between columns, recalls the Old Testament Wisdom figure who declares, 'The Lord possessed me at the beginning of his work' (Proverbs 8:22), as Mary bears the incarnate Wisdom of God. Saint Jerome at right, the great translator of the Latin Vulgate Bible, represents the Church's devotion to the written Word — his open book and scholarly posture remind viewers that access to Christ comes through the Scriptures he spent his life translating. The female saint at left, possibly a virgin martyr, represents the other pillar of Christian faithfulness: not scholarship but steadfast witness unto death. Together, the saints model the two dimensions of Christian life — active study and contemplative devotion — both oriented toward the Christ who is their center. The classical architectural setting suggests that in Christ, the wisdom of the ages finds its fulfillment.

scripture_refs.reason: Mary treasured all these things in her heart — the contemplative mother bearing the Word made flesh
The Word became flesh — the infant Christ held by Mary is the incarnate Logos
Wisdom present at creation — Mary as the seat of incarnate Wisdom
All Scripture is God-breathed — Jerome's lifelong devotion to the sacred text

_meta.title: The Virgin and Child with Saints Jerome and a Female Saint
